peer_exemplars.reasoning: Low trust in electoral process (implied by low political trust mean 40.11) and high populist anti-elitism (78.24) produce distrust in elections, but high system justification (84.95) partially counters this skepticism.

Direct electoral trust measure is not given but moderate trust in institutions (61% mean), high system justification (86%), and authoritarian conformity (69%) support basic trust, though low internal efficacy (9%) reduces confidence.
Low political efficacy (20.08) and moderate populist attitudes (54.96) combined with low system justification (27.96) create near-neutral trust, reflecting ambivalence about electoral integrity.
